entries.title: Pre-existing latent: stage_advance for produce creates schedule with empty chapter fields when next_chapter=None (novel-completion); surfaced by P1 fields but not introduced by them
entries.note: When next_chapter returns None (novel-completion per P0 selection), stage_advance for produce target still builds WorkFields with all chapter fields None and creates a schedule. The preset gates do not reject it. Does not regress completion or auto-enqueue (DF-53 deferred). Document that the schedule will be created but the writer prompt will see missing chapter context and should no-op or surface completion. Surface as completion UX in V1.39+.
entries.closure_note: V1.39 P5 reject_produce_when_novel_complete guard + 3 unit tests; re-confirmed V1.47 P2.

entries.title: Frontmatter field documentation removed from draft-chapter.md without replacement
entries.note: Old template contained a bulleted list explaining each frontmatter field (title, chapter, status, word_count, world_refs). New template drops this entirely; frontmatter example block remains but field descriptions are gone. Restore compact 1-2 line summary of required frontmatter fields in a follow-up.
entries.closure_note: V1.44 P3 T3: restored compact frontmatter field docs in draft-chapter.md — added bulleted list explaining title, chapter, status, word_count, and world_refs after the YAML example block. Commit 6b834ae8.

entries.title: _deprecated/ prompt files still embedded in binary via include_dir!; not loaded but occupy space
entries.source: QC1 S-2 / QC3 S2 (V1.38 P1 review); qc-consolidated.md
entries.note: Quarantined draft-body.md and draft-intro.md are still embedded in the binary at compile time via include_dir!. They are not referenced by preset.yaml. Either move _deprecated/ outside embedded-presets/ entirely, or configure include_dir! to exclude _deprecated/ patterns. Not blocking for this plan.

entries.title: outline_path / body_path required: true in templates with no defaults for non-CLI callers
entries.note: If a caller (e.g., e2e test, daemon, or future API) constructs preset.input directly without going through CLI stage_advance, the required: true constraint means template rendering fails. Current callers all populate via stage_advance. Add runtime validation in schedule builder that warns when required template vars are missing from preset.input. Document expanded preset.input contract in preset.yaml header comments.
entries.closure_note: V1.39 P5 accept: current callers all CLI flows. Register R-V139P5-N3 for V1.40 if non-CLI caller added.

entries.title: chapter_label for chapter >=100 yields "100" not "0100" (no fixed-width beyond 2 digits)
entries.note: Spec §4.5.6 accepts 2-digit zero-pad for chapter1-99; chapter100+ grows naturally to width. Document format contract in spec or add fixed-width if future novels require it. No correctness break for P1.

entries.title: O(n) linear scan over chapters[] in stage_advance (typical n<100, negligible)
entries.source: QC2 S-2 / QC3 S4 (V1.38 P1 review); qc-consolidated.md
entries.note: chapters.iter().find(...) is O(n). At typical novel scales (<=100 chapters) this is negligible. Add index if multi-volume works with 100+ chapters become common.

entries.title: stage_advance lacks audit logging for chapter context extraction (observability gap)
entries.note: Add tracing::info! or debug! span showing the selected chapter context (chapter_label, outline_path, body_path, slug) to aid production debugging when chapter selection behaves unexpectedly.
entries.closure_note: V1.44 P3 T4: added tracing::debug! span (target: fl_e.stage) after chapter context extraction in stage_advance(). Logs work_id, next_chapter, chapter_label, outline_path, body_path, and slug. Commit 93db2288.
